extractions.problem: Understanding the mechanism by which cytochrome P-450 reacts with polyhalomethanes in biodehalogenation, including products, stoichiometry, and kinetics.
extractions.method: Kinetic and stoichiometric analysis of cytochrome P-450 cam oxidation by polyhalomethanes, comparing isolated enzyme, deuteroheme, and whole-cell Pseudomonas putida (PpG-786) reactivity.
extractions.finding: All reactions proceed via hydrogenolysis of the carbon-halogen bond. P. putida is a valid whole-cell model for P-450 reductase activity. Iron-bonded carbenes are not intermediates.
extractions.dataset: Pseudomonas putida PpG-786, cytochrome P-450 cam
extractions.metric: reaction rate, stoichiometry
extractions.result: trichloronitromethane fastest, chloroform slowest substrate

extractions.problem: Evaluating whether inactivated whole-virus or Tween-ether-extracted FIV vaccines can protect against systemic and mucosal challenge in a feline AIDS model.
extractions.method: Immunization with whole inactivated virus (WIV) or Tween-ether viral protein (TEV) vaccines, followed by intravenous cell-free virus or intravaginal FIV-infected cell challenge in cats.
extractions.finding: Neither vaccine conferred resistance to systemic IV challenge, but WIV vaccination significantly reduced viral burden following intravaginal mucosal challenge despite similar infection rates.
extractions.dataset: feline immunodeficiency virus (FIV) cat model
extractions.metric: viraemia levels, infection rate
extractions.result: significantly lower viral burdens in WIV vaccinees after intravaginal challenge

extractions.problem: Understanding the role of secreted microRNAs as a novel form of intercellular communication in multicellular organisms.
extractions.method: Literature review and synthesis of studies on microvesicle-dependent and RNA-binding protein-associated miRNA trafficking between cells.
extractions.finding: Secreted miRNAs are actively trafficked between cells via microvesicles or RNA-binding proteins, functioning as endogenous miRNAs in recipient cells to regulate multiple target genes simultaneously.
extractions.dataset: multicellular organism intercellular communication

extractions.problem: Modeling skin heating and injury mechanisms from prolonged millimeter-wave electromagnetic radiation exposure, including both direct thermal and indirect biochemical injury.
extractions.method: Transport-lattice system model coupling a layered skin model with a whole-body model to estimate heat and chemical transport in rat skin in vivo at 94 GHz.
extractions.finding: The model's thermal response matches experimental measurements. Prolonged low-intensity exposure (75 mW/cm², 40 min) causes significant direct thermal injury, while short high-intensity exposure (1 W/cm², 10 s) causes less. Biochemical release may cause additional indirect injury at distant sites.
extractions.dataset: anesthetized rat skin in vivo, 94 GHz MMW exposure
extractions.metric: subcutaneous and colonic temperature, thermal-injury indicator
extractions.baseline: experimental temperature measurements from prior studies
extractions.result: model consistent with measured temperatures at 75 mW/cm² for 40-min exposure

extractions.problem: Addressing the shortage of donor organs for transplantation by developing whole-organ tissue engineering approaches using decellularized scaffolds.
extractions.method: Review of decellularization methods for vascular organs (heart, liver, lung) to produce acellular 3D biologic scaffolds, followed by recellularization with selected cell populations and bioreactor culture.
extractions.finding: Decellularized organ scaffolds provide naturally occurring 3D matrices that can be reseeded with cells. Preliminary animal model studies show proof of concept, but significant challenges remain.
extractions.dataset: heart, liver, lung donor organs; animal models
extractions.result: encouraging proof-of-concept results in animal models

extractions.problem: Understanding how catecholamine-induced changes in individual neuron responsivity translate to improved signal detection at the network level.
extractions.method: Neural network model of catecholamine effects combined with parallel distributed processing computer simulation of signal detection performance.
extractions.finding: Changes in individual neuron responsivity do not improve single-element signal detection, but the same changes in a network of elements do improve whole-network signal detection, accounting for CNS stimulant effects on human performance.
extractions.dataset: simulated neural network, human signal detection task
extractions.metric: signal detection performance, signal-to-noise ratio
extractions.result: network-level signal detection improvement from responsivity changes

extractions.problem: Developing a model to estimate leukocyte composition from whole-blood DNA methylation data using cell counts as reference.
extractions.method: Training a predictive model using whole-blood DNA methylation profiles with cell counts as reference.
extractions.finding: The model provided precise estimates for common cell types.
extractions.dataset: whole-blood DNA methylation, leukocyte cell counts
extractions.metric: estimation precision
extractions.result: precise estimates for common cell types

extractions.problem: Comparing the efficacy of syngeneic vs allogeneic cytokine-expressing whole-cell tumor vaccines and determining the influence of irradiation in a murine melanoma model.
extractions.method: Prophylactic vaccination of syngeneic C57/BL6 and allogeneic C3H mice with B16-F10 melanoma cells transfected with GM-CSF, IL-4, or IL-7, followed by tumor challenge. CTL assays and fluorescent dye tracking of vaccine cells.
extractions.finding: GM-CSF and IL-7 significantly protected syngeneic mice but not allogeneic mice. Irradiated vaccines of either tissue-type are rapidly processed, suggesting cytokine transfection effects are transient and of limited long-term use in situ.
extractions.dataset: B16-F10 murine melanoma, C57/BL6 and C3H mice
extractions.metric: survival, CTL activity, p-value
extractions.result: GM-CSF and IL-7 significant protection in syngeneic (P<0.05), not in allogeneic (P>0.05)

extractions.problem: Developing a general mathematical framework for simulating both single-channel and whole-cell ionic currents from arbitrary first-order kinetic schemes of ion channel conformational states.
extractions.method: Solution of the master equation (Markov process) expressed in terms of eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the kinetic system, with numerical implementation for complex schemes.
extractions.finding: The derived expression provides a general recipe for whole-cell current calculation and conditional probabilities for single-channel simulation, demonstrated with an activation-inactivation model.
extractions.dataset: ion channel kinetic models

extractions.problem: Characterizing the full spectrum of microglial activation states in neurodegeneration and identifying disease-relevant expression signatures not captured by mouse models alone.
extractions.method: Co-regulated gene module derivation from CNS myeloid transcriptional profiles across diverse mouse models, single-cell RNA-seq analysis, and whole-tissue RNA profiling from human neurodegenerative disease with cellular composition correction.
extractions.finding: Identified microglial subsets expressing interferon-related or proliferation modules distinct from previously reported disease-associated microglia. Human AD tissue showed elevated neurodegeneration modules plus modules not implicated by mouse models alone.
extractions.dataset: mouse tauopathy and AD models, human AD brain tissue
extractions.metric: gene module expression, single-cell clustering
extractions.result: identified novel microglial activation states and AD-specific modules absent in mouse models

extractions.problem: Proposing a model for how the number of repeated structures (e.g., somites) is controlled during animal morphogenesis.
extractions.dataset: animal morphogenesis, somitogenesis

extractions.problem: Determining the synaptic mechanisms underlying frequency and intensity tuning in auditory cortex neurons, particularly the role of inhibition.
extractions.method: In vivo whole-cell recordings from single neurons in rat auditory cortex to measure excitatory and inhibitory receptive fields and their temporal dynamics.
extractions.finding: Excitatory and inhibitory receptive fields overlap almost exactly, contradicting lateral inhibition models. Balanced inhibition rapidly truncates excitatory responses within 1-4 ms, increasing temporal precision rather than establishing tuning.
extractions.dataset: rat primary auditory cortex neurons
extractions.metric: receptive field overlap, spike timing precision
extractions.result: inhibition truncates spiking within 1-4 ms

extractions.problem: Characterizing endothelial cell gene expression changes in an animal model of early-onset Fuchs' endothelial corneal dystrophy (FECD).
extractions.method: Whole genome expression analysis of corneal endothelial cells in a mouse model of FECD.
extractions.finding: First endothelial whole genome expression analysis in an animal FECD model; identified COX2 up-regulation as warranting further investigation for its role in disease.
extractions.dataset: mouse model of early-onset FECD, corneal endothelial cells
extractions.metric: gene expression levels
extractions.result: endothelial COX2 up-regulation identified

extractions.problem: Developing dye-sensitized solar cells optimized for efficient power generation under ambient indoor lighting conditions.
extractions.dataset: dye-sensitized solar cells, ambient lighting

extractions.problem: Developing and characterizing a thin nano-porous bi-layer latex coating system to preserve Gluconobacter oxydans as a high-intensity whole-cell biocatalyst.
extractions.method: Entrapping G. oxydans in acrylate/vinyl acetate co-polymer coatings (<50 µm), measuring intrinsic Michaelis-Menten kinetics in microbioreactors, determining diffusivities via diffusion cell, and validating with a diffusion-reaction model.
extractions.finding: High reaction rates (80-100 g/L·h) achieved with cellcoat effectiveness factors of 0.22-0.49, which are 20-fold greater than previously reported. About 50% viability lost during drying.
extractions.dataset: Gluconobacter oxydans, D-sorbitol to L-sorbose oxidation
extractions.metric: reaction rate (g/L·h), effectiveness factor, viable cell density
extractions.baseline: previously reported G. oxydans whole-cell oxidation systems
extractions.result: 80-100 g/L·h reaction rate, 20-fold higher effectiveness factor

extractions.problem: Determining the detailed atomic structure of the membrane-associated Torpedo nicotinic acetylcholine receptor in the closed-channel form.
extractions.method: Cryo-electron microscopy of helical tubes (342 images) with crystallographic refinement of the density map placed in an artificial unit cell.
extractions.finding: Refined 4Å structure reveals the complete receptor including ligand-binding and intracellular domains. ACh-coordinating residues are far apart in the closed channel, and comparison with AChBP suggests how ligand binding initiates channel opening via loop closure and rotational movements.
extractions.dataset: Torpedo nicotinic acetylcholine receptor
extractions.metric: R-factor, R-free, resolution
extractions.baseline: previous lower-resolution structures, AChBP crystal structure
extractions.result: R-factor 36.7%, R-free 37.9% at 4Å resolution

extractions.problem: Characterizing the energy savings achievable by dynamically reducing active cells in cellular access networks during low-traffic periods.
extractions.method: Analytical models optimizing energy savings by switching off cells while maintaining coverage through remaining active cells, considering both continuous and discrete cell layout constraints.
extractions.finding: Significant energy savings are possible by reducing active cells during low-traffic periods while maintaining whole-area coverage through the remaining cells.
extractions.dataset: cellular access network models
extractions.metric: energy savings

extractions.problem: Determining whether the microtrabecular lattice observed in the cytoplasmic ground substance of cultured cells is a genuine structure or a specimen preparation artifact.
extractions.method: High voltage transmission electron microscopy of cultured cells (WI-38, NRK, chick embryo fibroblasts) with various fixation methods (chemical and freezing), critical-point drying validation, and comparison with plastic-embedded specimens.
extractions.finding: The microtrabecular lattice (3-6 nm strands forming a 3D network) is preserved under diverse fixation and drying methods and closely represents the nonrandom structure of living cytoplasmic ground substance, not an artifact.
extractions.dataset: WI-38, NRK, chick embryo fibroblast cultured cells; erythrocyte and protein solution models
extractions.metric: structural preservation across fixation methods
extractions.result: lattice preserved by chemical and nonchemical fixation; 3-6 nm diameter strands

extractions.problem: Validating Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH) as a drug target for novel anti-malarial chemotherapy.
extractions.method: High-throughput screening for PfDHODH inhibitors, selectivity profiling against human DHODH, whole-cell parasite assays, and lead optimization of triazolopyrimidine series with in vivo mouse model testing.
extractions.finding: Multiple chemical scaffolds show potent and selective PfDHODH inhibition with strong enzyme-to-parasite activity correlation. An optimized triazolopyrimidine analog is orally bioavailable with good in vivo efficacy, validating PfDHODH as a drug target.
extractions.dataset: Plasmodium falciparum, in vivo mouse malaria model
extractions.metric: enzyme inhibition potency, selectivity ratio, oral bioavailability, in vivo efficacy
extractions.result: strong selectivity for malarial over human enzyme; oral bioavailability and in vivo efficacy demonstrated

extractions.problem: Understanding how tumour cell heterogeneity from genetic mutations affects the geometry and invasive structure of solid tumours during early vascular growth.
extractions.method: Hybrid mathematical model combining discrete individual tumour cells with continuous concentrations of extracellular matrix, matrix-degradative enzymes, and oxygen, examining cell-cell and cell-matrix adhesion interactions.
extractions.finding: Individual cell interactions, particularly cell adhesion properties, critically influence tumour shape and invasive structure, with heterogeneous cell populations producing distinct geometric patterns.
extractions.dataset: simulated solid tumour invasion model
extractions.metric: tumour geometry, invasion patterns

extractions.problem: Resolving the inconsistency between discrete biome-based vegetation representation in climate models and the physiological parameterizations needed for biogeochemical coupling.
extractions.method: Simulations with NCAR LSM using satellite-derived (1 km) plant functional type (PFT) distributions as continuous patches within grid cells, compared at 3°, 2°, and 1° resolutions.
extractions.finding: Continuous PFT representation produces direct responses in ground temperature, evaporation, and albedo, reduces model sensitivity to spatial resolution, and provides a necessary bridge between climate and ecosystem models.
extractions.dataset: 1-km satellite vegetation data, NCAR LSM global simulations
extractions.metric: ground temperature, evaporation, albedo, sensible/latent heat flux
extractions.result: reduced model sensitivity to resolution with continuous PFT representation

extractions.problem: Proposing a mechanosensory transduction mechanism by which osteocytes sense small in vivo bone strains through fluid shear stresses on their cell processes.
extractions.method: Biot's porous media theory combined with fiber-filled channel flow theory to model fluid flow through proteoglycan-filled canalicular annuli surrounding osteocytic processes, relating whole-bone loads to local fluid shear.
extractions.finding: Predicted pore relaxation time (1-2 s) matches experimental measurements, and predicted fluid shear stresses (8-30 dyn/cm²) match levels known to elicit intracellular Ca²⁺ responses. The canalicular pore space fiber spacing is ~7 nm.
extractions.dataset: osteocyte canalicular system, bone loading models
extractions.metric: pore pressure relaxation time, fluid shear stress
extractions.baseline: previous predictions (100-fold too short relaxation times)
extractions.result: relaxation time 1-2 s, shear stress 8-30 dyn/cm², fiber spacing ~7 nm

extractions.problem: Testing whether polyploidy (whole-genome duplication) can accelerate the rate of evolutionary adaptation.
extractions.method: In vitro evolution experiments comparing haploid, diploid, and tetraploid yeast, with mathematical modeling, whole-genome sequencing, and phenotypic analysis of evolved clones.
extractions.finding: Tetraploids adapt significantly faster than haploids and diploids, driven by higher rates of beneficial mutations with stronger fitness effects including aneuploidy, chromosome loss, and point mutations.
extractions.dataset: Saccharomyces cerevisiae haploid/diploid/tetraploid strains
extractions.metric: adaptation rate, fitness effects of mutations
extractions.result: tetraploids significantly faster adaptation

extractions.problem: Extending whole-powder-pattern fitting without a structural model (Pawley method) from neutron to X-ray powder diffraction data.
extractions.method: Individual profile fitting to establish angle-dependent profile-shape model, then whole-powder-pattern fitting with unit-cell-constrained peak positions and independent integrated intensities, applied to α-SiO₂, α-Al₂O₃, CaCO₃, and mixed ZrO₂.
extractions.finding: Peak shift correction has the largest influence on fit quality. Asymmetry and angular-dependent intensity decay must be modeled. The method was successfully applied to mixed monoclinic-tetragonal ZrO₂.
extractions.dataset: α-SiO₂, α-Al₂O₃, CaCO₃, mixed monoclinic/tetragonal ZrO₂
extractions.metric: overall pattern fit quality, refined parameter accuracy
extractions.result: successful whole-pattern fitting of X-ray data including mixed-phase ZrO₂

extractions.problem: Developing a theory for how cellular organization evolved, particularly the transition from communal horizontal gene transfer-dominated evolution to vertically inherited lineages.
extractions.method: Theoretical model based on the conjecture that horizontal gene transfer (HGT) dynamics are determined by recipient cell organization, with analysis of the transition from communal to lineage-based evolution.
extractions.finding: Aboriginal cells were simple enough for HGT to alter all componentry, making communal evolution the primary driver. As cell designs became more complex, a 'Darwinian Threshold' was crossed where vertical inheritance became dominant.
extractions.dataset: theoretical model of early cellular evolution

extractions.problem: Testing whether hydrostatic pressure can be locally regulated in animal cells, contradicting models that predict instantaneous pressure equilibration across the whole cell.
extractions.method: Using cell blebs as local pressure reporters, locally perfusing cortex-relaxing drugs on one side of blebbing cells, and modeling cytoplasm as a poroelastic contractile network.
extractions.finding: Pressure does not equilibrate across the cell on scales of ~10 µm and ~10 s. A poroelastic model where cytosol flows through a contractile elastic network accounts for spatially heterogeneous pressure transients.
extractions.dataset: blebbing animal cells
extractions.metric: pressure equilibration length and timescale
extractions.baseline: incompressible viscoelastic continuum / viscous liquid models
extractions.result: non-equilibration on scales of ~10 µm and ~10 s

extractions.problem: Developing a tractable analytical framework for downlink SINR analysis in heterogeneous cellular networks with flexible, biased cell association policies.
extractions.method: Multi-tier stochastic geometry model with randomly located base stations characterized by transmit power, path loss exponent, spatial density, and user admission bias; Cox proportional hazards-like derivations for outage and rate.
extractions.finding: Neither the number of BSs nor tiers changes outage probability or average ergodic rate in interference-limited full-loaded HCNs with unbiased association. Biasing alters performance metrics in predictable ways.
extractions.dataset: heterogeneous cellular network model (macro/pico/femtocells)
extractions.metric: outage probability, SINR CDF, average ergodic rate, minimum average user throughput
extractions.result: closed-form SINR distributions; outage invariant to BS count without biasing

extractions.problem: Elucidating the cellular and synaptic mechanisms by which patient antibodies cause anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis.
extractions.method: Application of patient NMDAR antibodies and Fab fragments to cultured rat hippocampal neurons with surface receptor quantification, whole-cell patch-clamp recordings, and in vivo antibody infusion in Lewis rats.
extractions.finding: Patient antibodies cause selective, titer-dependent, reversible decrease in NMDAR surface density via antibody-mediated capping and internalization, specifically reducing NMDAR-mediated synaptic currents without affecting AMPA receptors or other synaptic proteins.
extractions.dataset: patient NMDAR antibodies, cultured rat hippocampal neurons, female Lewis rats
extractions.metric: NMDAR surface density, miniature EPSC amplitude, antibody titer correlation
extractions.baseline: Fab fragments (no cross-linking), AMPA receptor currents
extractions.result: selective NMDAR decrease correlating with antibody titer; no effect on AMPA currents

extractions.problem: Reviewing the molecular genetics of T-cell antigen receptor genes and understanding the mechanisms of MHC-restricted antigen recognition.
extractions.method: Review of cloning and structural analysis of TCR alpha, beta, and gamma chain genes, including organization, ontogeny, rearrangement patterns, and diversification mechanisms.
extractions.finding: TCR genes follow the immunoglobulin paradigm in structure and diversification, with important differences. The rules governing T- and B-cell activation differ, and TCR ligands remain poorly characterized.
extractions.dataset: T-cell receptor alpha, beta, gamma genes

extractions.problem: Minimizing current redistribution inequality in gate-commutated thyristors (GCTs) caused by uneven impedances along the current path from GCT circles to the gate contact.
extractions.method: Whole GCT wafer hybrid model combining physics-based cell models with lumped parameter models, optimized using Tabu search heuristic algorithm with physical concentric contact rings for impedance measurement.
extractions.finding: An optimized set of GCT cell numbers for a 4-inch wafer was obtained, reducing current redistribution inequality by 20%.
extractions.dataset: 4-inch GCT wafer
extractions.metric: current redistribution inequality
extractions.result: 20% reduction in current redistribution inequality

extractions.problem: Determining the synaptic locus (presynaptic vs postsynaptic) of long-term potentiation (LTP) expression in hippocampal CA1.
extractions.method: Whole-cell voltage clamp recordings in hippocampal slices with statistical analysis of synaptic variability, including failure rate analysis and postsynaptic dialysis experiments.
extractions.finding: LTP involves presynaptic enhancement of transmitter release, as evidenced by decreased trial-to-trial variability and reduced proportion of synaptic failures. LTP triggering is postsynaptic but expression is presynaptic.
extractions.dataset: rat hippocampal CA1 slices, MGN-LA pathway
extractions.metric: synaptic variability, failure rate, EPSC amplitude
extractions.result: significant decrease in synaptic failures during LTP

extractions.problem: Evaluating the mouse intranasal/nasopharyngeal challenge model as a potency and release assay for whole-cell pertussis (wP) vaccines.
extractions.method: Mouse nasopharyngeal challenge model for testing whole-cell pertussis vaccine potency.
extractions.finding: The mouse nasopharyngeal challenge model could serve as a potency and release assay for wP vaccines, though direct correlation with clinical vaccine efficacy requires further investigation.
extractions.dataset: mouse nasopharyngeal challenge model, whole-cell pertussis vaccines
extractions.metric: potency

extractions.problem: Understanding the molecular and cellular mechanisms of the epithelial to mesenchymal transition (EMT) and its relevance to cancer metastasis and organ fibrosis.
extractions.method: Review of transcription factor regulatory networks, posttranscriptional and epigenetic programs, and developmental EMT studies as models for pathological EMT.
extractions.finding: EMT is now established as critical in the metastatic cascade, regulated by complex transcriptional loops coupled to epigenetic programs, and linked to cell survival, stemness, inflammation, and immunity. It constitutes a promising therapeutic target.
extractions.dataset: epithelial tumors, organ fibrosis models, embryonic development

extractions.problem: Sequencing the genome of moso bamboo, the first species in the Bambusoideae subfamily, to understand its biology and rapid growth.
extractions.method: Whole-genome shotgun sequencing and assembly, gene prediction with cDNA and deep RNA-seq support, clustered gene family analysis, and collinearity analysis for whole-genome duplication detection.
extractions.finding: The 2.05-Gb assembly (95% coverage) contains 31,987 genes. Bamboo underwent whole-genome duplication 7-12 MYA, generating gene duplicates involved in shoot development. A potential connection between drought-responsive and flowering genes was identified.
extractions.dataset: Phyllostachys heterocycla var. pubescens (moso bamboo)
extractions.metric: genome coverage, gene count
extractions.result: 2.05-Gb assembly, 95% coverage, 31,987 genes, WGD 7-12 MYA

extractions.problem: Understanding how unconnected glioblastoma cells, lacking tumor microtube network connections, contribute to whole-brain colonization and invasion.
extractions.method: In vivo imaging, single-cell transcriptomics, calcium imaging, and analysis of tumor cell migration patterns (Lévy-like movement) in mouse models and human glioblastoma samples.
extractions.finding: Unconnected glioblastoma cells in neuronal/neural-progenitor-like states receive synaptic input from neurons, use neuronal migration mechanisms, and are driven by neuronal activity to form new tumor microtubes and increase invasion speed.
extractions.dataset: mouse glioblastoma models, human glioblastoma tissue, single-cell transcriptomics
extractions.metric: invasion speed, calcium signal complexity, Lévy-like movement patterns
extractions.result: neuronal activity increases invasion speed and de novo TM formation

extractions.problem: Quantifying memory CD8 T cell number, distribution, and recovery biases across tissues to reassess immunosurveillance models.
extractions.method: Parabiosis and quantitative immunofluorescence microscopy of mouse memory CD8 T cells, comparing results with standard lymphocyte isolation methods.
extractions.finding: Standard isolation methods fail to recover most cells and bias against certain subsets. Resident memory T cells greatly outnumber recirculating cells in non-lymphoid tissues, and homing patterns do not conform to hypothesized migration models.
extractions.dataset: mouse memory CD8 T cell population across tissues
extractions.metric: cell recovery rate, resident vs recirculating cell ratio
extractions.result: residents greatly outnumber recirculating cells in non-lymphoid tissues

extractions.problem: Determining whether lower vascular-normalizing doses of anti-VEGFR2 therapy can reprogram the immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment to enhance cancer vaccine immunotherapy.
extractions.method: Comparison of low (normalizing) vs high (antivascular) doses of anti-VEGFR2 antibody in combination with whole cancer cell vaccine in murine breast cancer models, with macrophage polarization and T-cell infiltration analysis.
extractions.finding: Lower normalizing doses are superior to high doses in promoting M2-to-M1 macrophage polarization, CD4+/CD8+ T-cell infiltration, and enhancing vaccine efficacy in a CD8+ T-cell-dependent manner.
extractions.dataset: immune-tolerant and immunogenic murine breast cancer models
extractions.metric: macrophage M1/M2 polarization, T-cell infiltration, anticancer efficacy
extractions.result: lower doses superior in M1 polarization, T-cell infiltration, and vaccine efficacy

extractions.problem: Designing a self-organizing multilayered neural network (Cognitron) capable of unsupervised pattern recognition.
Testing the cancer multi-hit model prediction that normal individuals carry stable populations of cancer-prone mutant cells by searching for p53-mutated keratinocyte clones in normal human skin.
extractions.method: Whole-mount preparations of human skin with immunohistochemical detection of p53-mutated keratinocyte clones, comparing sun-exposed and sun-shielded skin.
extractions.finding: Normal skin contains frequent clonal patches of p53-mutated keratinocytes (60-3000 cells) at >40 cells/cm², covering up to 4% of epidermis. Sun-exposed skin has more frequent and larger clones, showing sunlight acts as both mutagen and tumor promoter.
extractions.dataset: normal human skin (sun-exposed and sun-shielded)
extractions.metric: clone frequency (cells/cm²), clone size, epidermal coverage (%)
extractions.result: >40 clones/cm², up to 4% epidermis, clones 60-3000 cells

extractions.problem: Identifying prognostic factors for overall survival in metastatic renal cell carcinoma patients treated with VEGF-targeted agents.
extractions.method: Retrospective analysis of 645 anti-VEGF therapy-naïve mRCC patients from 7 centers using Cox proportional hazards regression with bootstrap validation.
extractions.finding: Six independent prognostic factors identified (4 MSKCC factors + neutrophils + platelets > ULN). Three risk groups: favorable (mOS not reached, 2y OS 75%), intermediate (mOS 27 mo, 2y OS 53%), poor (mOS 8.8 mo, 2y OS 7%).
extractions.dataset: 645 metastatic RCC patients from 7 US/Canadian centers
extractions.metric: overall survival, C-index, hazard ratio
extractions.result: C-index 0.73; mOS: favorable NR, intermediate 27 mo, poor 8.8 mo

extractions.problem: Resolving the debate over whether long-term potentiation (LTP) expression in hippocampal CA1 involves presynaptic or postsynaptic modification.
extractions.method: Whole-cell voltage clamp recordings in hippocampal slices with analysis of synaptic variability, failure rates, and effects of prolonged postsynaptic dialysis on LTP triggering vs expression.
extractions.finding: LTP is robustly recorded with improved signal resolution. Postsynaptic dialysis blocks LTP triggering but not expression. Changes in synaptic variability and decreased failures during LTP indicate significant presynaptic enhancement of transmitter release.
extractions.dataset: rat hippocampal CA1 slices
extractions.metric: synaptic variability, failure proportion, signal-to-noise ratio
extractions.baseline: pre-LTP baseline responses, conventional intracellular recordings
extractions.result: decreased failure proportion and variability during LTP

extractions.problem: Providing a comprehensive reference on the theory and applications of dislocations in materials science.
Understanding how phosphatidylcholine (PC) and phosphatidylethanolamine (PE) metabolism regulates lipid, lipoprotein, and whole-body energy metabolism and their roles in disease.
extractions.method: Review of dietary studies and knockout animal models examining the effects of altered PC/PE ratios on energy metabolism, lipid droplet dynamics, mitochondrial function, and disease progression.
extractions.finding: Abnormal PC/PE ratios in various tissues influence energy metabolism and are linked to fatty liver disease, impaired liver regeneration, atherosclerosis, insulin resistance, and obesity. Hepatic PC synthesis impairment disrupts VLDL secretion.
extractions.dataset: dietary studies, knockout animal models, mammalian cell membranes
extractions.metric: PC/PE molar ratio, VLDL secretion, lipid droplet size
extractions.result: abnormal PC/PE ratios linked to fatty liver, atherosclerosis, insulin resistance, obesity

extractions.problem: Developing a mathematical model linking bursting electrical activity in pancreatic β-cells to whole-body glucose-insulin dynamics.
extractions.dataset: pancreatic β-cells, glucose-insulin regulatory system

extractions.problem: Understanding the regional heterogeneity of the mammalian sinoatrial node in terms of cellular properties, ionic currents, gap junctions, and its functional implications for cardiac pacemaking.
extractions.method: Review of experimental studies from single SA node cells to whole animals, examining morphology, action potential configuration, ionic current densities, connexin expression, and autonomic regulation.
extractions.finding: SA node heterogeneity (gradient or mosaic model) is essential for dependable pacemaking: it allows the SA node to drive atrial muscle without electrotonic suppression, promotes antegrade propagation, prevents reentry, and enables pacemaker shift under pathophysiological conditions.
extractions.dataset: mammalian sinoatrial node, single cells to whole animal
extractions.metric: ionic current densities, connexin expression, action potential configuration
extractions.result: heterogeneity enables electrotonic resistance, antegrade propagation, and pacemaker shift
